scaled_oracle_cases.description: Transformed seed case: verify exact error profile values and determinism, preserving original print and file save behavior.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Returned list must exactly match [1, 0.06, 0.02, 0.02, 0.01, 0.005, 0.005, 0.005, 0.001, 0.001, 0.001, 0.0005] and two consecutive calls return equal but not identical lists.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Returning wrong values, same mutable object, or non-deterministic outputs.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
profile = get_default_error_profile()
assert isinstance(profile, list), "Profile should be a list"
assert len(profile) == 12, "Profile should contain 12 entries"
assert profile == [1, 0.06, 0.02, 0.02, 0.01, 0.005, 0.005, 0.005, 0.001, 0.001, 0.001, 0.0005], "Profile does not match expected values"
# Determinism and independence check
profile2 = get_default_error_profile()
assert profile == profile2, "Profiles are not equal"
assert profile is not profile2, "Profiles are the same object"
print("Default Illumina error profile:", profile)
with open("error_profile.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(profile, f)
print("Profile saved successfully to error_profile.json")
result = profile
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the exact value requirements and determinism requirement.
Solutions must satisfy stronger validated oracle case coverage.
